name: Guadiana Nature Trail
name_original: Embalse de Vicario por los Baños del Emperador o Trujillo — circular por el Río Guadiana
description: Hike a moderate 7.8-mile circular route around Embalse de Vicario, featuring Roman baths, an iron bridge, and excellent birdwatching.
region_intro: Hiking around Ciudad Real offers diverse landscapes, from extensive oak and holm oak forests to unique wetland ecosystems and volcanic formations. The region is characterized by natural parks such as Cabañeros, known for its rugged mountains and riverine scenery, and Las Tablas de Daimiel, featuring freshwater lagoons. Volcanic activity has shaped areas like the Laguna and Volcán de La Posadilla, providing distinct geological features for exploration. Best hiking trails in Ciudad Real The most popular hiking route is Guadiana Nature Trail, a 7.8 miles (12.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 14 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a section of the historic GR 114, offering views of the Guadiana River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Alarcos Trail, a moderate 8.8 miles (14.2 km) path. This trail explores the general countryside around Ciudad Real, with an estimated completion time of 3 hours 40 minutes. Local hikers also love the Posadilla Volcano and Lagoon Loop, a 6.2 miles (9.9 km) trail leading through a unique volcanic landscape, often completed in about 2 hours 54 minutes. Hiking around Ciudad Real is defined by volcanic features, extensive holm oak forests, and wetland lagoons. The network offers options for various ability levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Ciudad Real are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Ciudad Real's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Ciudad Real, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: Ciudad Real offers a diverse network of over 90 hiking routes. These include 43 easy trails, 53 moderate routes, and 2 more challenging options, ensuring there's something for every hiker's ability.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails in Ciudad Real?
A: Yes, Ciudad Real has several excellent options for families. The trails within &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3882572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Tablas de Daimiel National Park&lt;/a&gt; are generally flat and ideal for birdwatching, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2661834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; offers beautiful scenery and opportunities for swimming and kayaking alongside easy walks.

Q: What kind of unique natural features can I expect to see on hikes in Ciudad Real?
A: Ciudad Real is known for its unique geological diversity. You can explore fascinating volcanic landscapes, such as those found on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197977248/posadilla-volcano-and-lagoon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Posadilla Volcano and Lagoon Loop&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the unique wetland ecosystems of Las Tablas de Daimiel. The Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park also features a stunning series of stepped lagoons connected by waterfalls.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Ciudad Real?
A: Yes, many trails in Ciudad Real are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197977248/posadilla-volcano-and-lagoon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Posadilla Volcano and Lagoon Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through a unique volcanic landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1201520350/ascent-to-campo-de-calatrava-circular-from-ciudad-real" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Campo de Calatrava — circular from Ciudad Real&lt;/a&gt; also offers a great loop experience.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls in Ciudad Real?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2661834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is famous for its series of lagoons connected by natural waterfalls, creating a stunning visual spectacle. Additionally, the Las Lastras and La Batanera Trail is known to lead to waterfalls and unique rock formations.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I visit while hiking in Ciudad Real?
A: Hiking in Ciudad Real can be combined with exploring rich history. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3882572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcos Archaeological Park&lt;/a&gt;, which is near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1198513805/alarcos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcos Trail&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2661834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calatrava la Vieja Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features several castles, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3489286" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of the Ciruela Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5631925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Caracuel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Ciudad Real?
A: The trails in Ciudad Real are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from the extensive holm oak forests to the unique volcanic features and tranquil wetland lagoons, offering varied and engaging experiences.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting on hikes in Ciudad Real?
A: Absolutely. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3882572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabañeros National Park&lt;/a&gt; is renowned for its rich flora and fauna, including deer and wild boar. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2661834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Tablas de Daimiel National Park&lt;/a&gt; is a prime location for birdwatching, home to flamingos, herons, and otters. The Valle de Alcudia also serves as a refuge for numerous species.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Ciudad Real?
A: The best seasons for hiking in Ciudad Real are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these months, the weather is milder, and the natural landscapes are at their most vibrant, with pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be chilly, though hiking is possible year-round with appropriate preparation.

Q: Are there any long-distance or historical routes in Ciudad Real?
A: Yes, Ciudad Real is part of significant historical routes. You can explore sections of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1201502677/guadiana-nature-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana Nature Trail&lt;/a&gt; (GR 114), which follows the historic river. The GR 164 Cañada Real Conquense, an ancient transhumance route, also passes through the province, offering a glimpse into the region's cultural heritage. Additionally, the famous Don Quixote's Route traverses the area.

Q: Where can I find caves or volcanic features to explore near Ciudad Real?
A: Ciudad Real's volcanic past is evident in several sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197977248/posadilla-volcano-and-lagoon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Posadilla Volcano and Lagoon Loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you to a fascinating geological formation. For caves, you can explore highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5047946" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Shepherd's Cave&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6322053" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Minas El Chorrillo Prospecting Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;, offering unique underground experiences.
tips: Iron bridge of the Madrid-Atocha to Badajoz-Frontera railway line, inaugurated in 1927 on the old route from Fernán Caballero to Ciudad Real, declared a historic-artistic monument.

Peralvillo Bridge, from which you can see the old Roman bridge.
Magnificent views of the Vicario Reservoir. Very close by The Hermitage of San Marcos Restored in November 2020. Without a doubt it is one more opportunity to visit the village of Peralvillo and get to know the extraordinary natural environment that surrounds it.
Viewpoint with wooden walkways as an observatory for birds. Passing the Peralvillo bridge on the left.

Next to the Trujillo farm, we find the old Roman road, of which now only a few stones remain, to access “Los Baños del Emperador”, also known as “Los Baños de Trujillo”. These are two small stone pools from Roman times, now surrounded by undergrowth and in the center of a small islet. One of them was for men and the other, smaller, was for women. The "hotbeds" are formed when the accumulation of gases (CO2) emerge and mix with the water of the springs, giving rise to these thermal pools.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Embalse de Vicario circular trail?
faqs.answer: This circular route is considered to be of moderate difficulty. While it requires good physical fitness, many paths are easily accessible, making it suitable for various skill levels. The total distance is approximately 12.6 km with about 60 meters of elevation gain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied terrain, including serene waterside paths along the Embalse de El Vicario, agricultural paths through fields of cereals, vineyards, and olive groves, and sections with characteristic Mediterranean vegetation. Some parts may be sinuous, following the natural curves of the landscape.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll discover a blend of natural beauty and historical sites. Key highlights include the ancient Emperor's Baths (Baños del Emperador), the Iron Bridge over the Guadiana River, a Roman Bridge, and the Vicario Reservoir Viewpoint. The Emperor's Baths are particularly unique, featuring Roman-era stone pools with naturally bubbling water due to underground volcanic activity.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I expect to see, especially birds?
faqs.answer: The Embalse de El Vicario is a crucial wetland ecosystem, making it an exceptional spot for birdwatching. You can expect to see a wide variety of resident and migratory birds, including ducks, flamingos, cormorants, coots, plovers, imperial herons, marsh harriers, and stilts. Bird observatories are strategically placed along the route to enhance your viewing opportunities.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable throughout much of the year, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking and optimal conditions for birdwatching. During drought periods, the reservoir becomes even more vital for birds, increasing your chances of spotting diverse species.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Embalse de Vicario trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, the route often starts or passes through Peralvillo, near the Ermita de San Marcos. Given its proximity to Ciudad Real (about 12 kilometers), there are likely parking options available in or near Peralvillo, which serves as a common access point for trails around the reservoir.

faqs.question: Are there public transport options to reach the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail is located about 12 kilometers from Ciudad Real. While direct public transport to the immediate trailhead might be limited, you can likely find bus services from Ciudad Real to nearby towns like Peralvillo or Miguelturra, from where you might need to arrange a short taxi ride or walk to the starting point.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de Vicario trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as parts of the route pass through natural areas and agricultural land, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially to protect local wildlife and respect private property.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this area?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Refugio de Fauna Embalse de El Vicario. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking on the public paths within this natural refuge. However, always respect any posted regulations regarding access, especially concerning protected wildlife areas.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other well-known paths. You will hike along parts of the Camino Natural del Guadiana, the Camino Manchego de Santiago, and the CAMINO NATURAL GUADIANA GR 114. It also briefly touches upon the Senda Carrera del Caballo and the puente de hierro (Iron Bridge) trail.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is conveniently located close to Ciudad Real, which offers a wide range of cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. Smaller villages like Peralvillo and Miguelturra, near the reservoir, may also have local establishments for refreshments or a meal.

waypoints.name: Iron Bridge over the Guadiana River
